hook_patterns: Every student asks this one question — [specific anxiety]. But trust me, the real answer depends on [unexpected factor].
Do you know that most students think [common belief]? But [surprising truth].
See, almost every student panics when [specific situation]. And suddenly you're like, [relatable inner monologue].
If you belong to [specific group] and you're dreaming of [goal], but [obstacle] is the only thing stopping you, then trust me, this video could change everything for you.
Everyone talks about [topic], but you know what's actually crazy? Most Indian students end up missing [specific thing]. Not because they're not smart enough, but simply because [real reason].
transition_phrases: So let's break this down properly.
So what should you actually do?
And here is what most students miss —
Let me be very honest with you —
Now let me say something honestly.
And this is exactly where most students get confused.
So make sure that you remember —
So, how do you [X]? It's not really about [Y], it's about [Z].
Most Indian students don't [X] properly. And honestly, that's why they end up [negative outcome].
sentence_rhythm: Alternates short punchy facts (6-10 words) with one explanatory sentence (18-25 words). Starts sentences with 'So', 'And', 'But' deliberately. Uses 3 short beats then one longer explanation. Pattern: [Short fact]. [Short fact]. But here's the thing — [longer explanation with real consequence]. Never more than 3 sentences before shifting to a new beat.
data_framing_style: Never drops a number alone. Always frames it with emotional consequence for the student. Pattern: '[Bank] can take longer — specifically during peak visa months. If you're rushing for a Fall intake, that delay can really stress you out.' OR 'The total cost is around 25 to 30 lakhs. But here is what most students miss — lower fees don't automatically mean easier loan approval.' Always converts financial jargon into student-life impact immediately.
cta_pattern: Never aggressive. Always empathetic. Pattern: 'So if you are planning for [goal], don't stress out. Start early, compare properly, and trust me — your future self will thank you.' OR 'If you are confused, don't stress at all. At EpiCred, we compare lenders officially based on your academic profile, co-applicant income, country and timeline. You'll know exactly which lender approves you faster, which one is cheaper, and which one is safest for your visa.' Ends with encouragement, never pressure.
empathy_phrases: I know it feels like the rules have completely changed overnight.
Don't stress — this is completely normal.
Trust me, your future self will thank you.
You know how parents panic in these moments.
Students stop sleeping because every hour suddenly feels important.
So please don't stress. We are here to simplify this for you.
Don't apply blindly. Structure planning will save you from rejections.
I hope you learned a lot from this session.
three_example_sentences: Every student asks this one question — which bank is actually best for my education loan? SBI, ICICI, Credila? And your parents' favourite — beta, go with the public bank, it's the safest option. But trust me, the real answer depends on things students usually don't check.
Most Indian students do not compare these things properly. And honestly, that's why they end up paying lakhs more over the entire loan tenure.
So if you are confused, don't stress at all. At EpiCred, we compare these lenders officially and side by side based on your academic profile, co-applicant income, country and timeline.
storytelling_patterns: Names a real student (e.g., 'Rohit from Pune') and walks through their exact problem step-by-step
Opens with the student's panic moment, then shows how it was solved
Uses 'Day 1... Day 2... Day 3...' timeline for dramatic pacing
Ends stories with the student's emotional relief quote
rhetorical_questions: You know why most students get confused?
So how should you choose the right [X]?
And why are comments flooding right now?
Do you know what [specific thing] actually means?
audience_address: Always uses 'you' and 'your' — never third person. Addresses the viewer as a younger sibling who needs clear guidance. Uses 'students' when talking about common mistakes. Never says 'guys' or 'friends' or 'viewers'.
self_corrections: Let me be very honest with you —
No wait, here's what I mean —
closing_patterns: So that's all for today. See you in the next video. Thank you for watching.
I hope you learned a lot from this session. If you have any queries, do reach out to the team.
Start early, compare properly, and trust me — your future self will thank you.
Don't stress but don't apply blindly either. Structure planning will save you from rejections.
